note: Mapped full render path. GameCard -> JerseyIcon (memo) -> per-sport wrapper (memo) -> MiniJerseyModel (memo, via in-progress edit). CompactLiveCard -> TeamJerseyCompact (memo). All deterministic by (team,sport): colors from getTeamColors, number from jerseyNumber(abbr) hash, wordmark from teamWordmark. No runtime SVG filters. No per-frame work inside SVG; Reanimated lift/shadow lives in wrapping containers only.

technique: One diagonal white linear-gradient def (`ids.sheen`) clipped to the body via an existing clip group, added to both the generic ModelDefs path and the BasketballSleevelessModel inline Defs. Bakes a directional shoulder->chest highlight; no feGaussianBlur, no filter.
node_delta_per_jersey: +1 gradient def (5 Stops) and +1 clipped Rect
per_frame_work: none — purely declarative gradient+rect, evaluated once at mount/memoized
mount_cost: negligible; gradient resolution is constant-time, no extra JS loops, no new array maps
device_fps: NOT MEASURED — no live device-FPS capture in this environment; reasoned about cost instead per no-mock-data rule
tests: JerseyVisualRefinement.test.ts 4/4 pass; tsc --noEmit clean on jerseyVisuals.tsx
gate: QUEUED — looks more real (curved-cloth specular cue) and holds budget (+2 nodes/jersey, zero per-frame cost, no filters, still memoized + deterministic)

technique: Added REDUCED_DETAIL_THRESHOLD=40; reducedDetail=size<40 in both MiniJerseyModel and BasketballSleevelessModel. Below threshold, skip sub-pixel layers: TextureLayer (weave), ClothFoldLayer, PanelVolume fine fold strokes, basketball channels/side-stitches/inner-panel strokes. Kept silhouette, body gradient, volume/edge/sheen rects, large side+center AO fills, collar/trim construction, rim/outline, glass, lettering.
visibility_proof: size 34 -> viewBox scale 0.34 px/unit; mesh dot r=0.45u renders ~0.31px, fold stroke 0.55u ~0.19px, both at/below sub-pixel floor at 0.07-0.13 opacity = invisible
large_sizes: identical to pre-change (else branch unchanged) at size>=40

gate: QUEUED — looks identical at 34px (dropped layers are sub-pixel) and large detail untouched, while the most-numerous jerseys mount far fewer (and fewer layout-heavy) nodes

technique: Replaced the single flat collar arc + flat AO ellipse with a modeled ribbed crew: inner-neck shadow path (depth into opening), rib trim band, top fold-edge highlight stroke, under-rib seam stroke. Same fill+dark+light technique as the existing basketball/cricket V-necks.
reason: weakest reads-as-cloth element of the 9 — only sport with a flat painted-on collar
device_fps: NOT MEASURED — reasoned about cost per no-mock-data rule
gate: QUEUED — neck now reads as folded ribbed cloth with a shadowed opening (more real) for +3 static nodes, zero per-frame cost

technique: Added reducedDetail to EmbroideredLabel; below threshold collapse the 10-pass twill (9 SvgText + 1 arc Path + stitch) to 3 passes that read at 34px: depth cast-shadow, integrated outline, fill. Drop arc shadow, surface shadow/highlight, inset, cloth-grain dash, highlight, stitch dash (all sub-pixel at 34px). Threaded flag through GarmentMarkings and the basketball inline label calls.
node_reduction_at_34px: ~7-14 fewer nodes per thumbnail (1-2 labels), and these are SvgText/glyph-layout nodes — the most expensive type — so mount saving is disproportionate
device_fps: NOT MEASURED — reasoned about cost per no-mock-data rule

gate: QUEUED — identical legible number/wordmark at 34px, full twill at large sizes, drops the costliest invisible passes on the most-numerous jerseys

technique: considered warping front number / nameplate baseline along a chest curve via textPath
discard_reason: believable sagitta ~1.5-2u over ~25u word = ~0.9-1.2px bow at size 60, ~0.5-0.7px at 34 — below visible threshold; textPath adds a Defs path per label and breaks the tuned multi-line fittedLabelLayout anchor model
gate: DISCARDED — no meaningful realism gain at real sizes, real cost+risk; existing per-sport rotation already gives the arc cue

technique: Added labelArcPath(cx,baselineY,halfWidth,arc) building ONE quadratic arc per line; gave EmbroideredLabel arc/arcId props and centralised all 10 twill passes through a single glyph() helper that emits flat <SvgText x/y> OR <SvgText><TextPath href startOffset=50%> when arcActive (arc!=0 && single line && arcId). One text element per pass either way, so an arced nameplate costs the same node count as a flat one (no per-glyph multiplication).
fixes: Deepak #1 complaint — prior 'arc' was a rotation prop (cricket -10, tennis -6, baseball -5) that tilted the word = crooked, not curved
arcid_namespacing: arc_${ids.clip}_word — unique per mounted jersey, no Path-id collisions across cards

technique: Tennis: rebuilt neck as a real polo — turn-down collar (two pointed wings + centre notch on an inner-neck shadow) + short button placket (fold-shaded strip + 2 buttons); removed the 2 soccer-style diagonal side seams. Cricket: added open turn-down collar wings over the V-neck so it reads as a collared cricket shirt.
reason: tennis was the weakest silhouette — a near-copy of the soccer kit; cricket spec calls for a collared colored jersey

technique: Added a single letterSpacing={tracking} attribute to the shared glyph() helper so every twill pass is set with the same negative optical tracking (-fontSize*0.03 for <=3-char marks/numerals, 0.022 <=6, 0.014 longer). Block/varsity jersey lettering is set tight; default spacing was the amateur tell. Lives in the ONE shared helper so all plies stay in perfect registration. Negative tracking => rendered width slightly under the estimator, can never overflow the fitted maxWidth.
device_fps: NOT MEASURED — no live device-FPS capture; reasoned about cost per no-mock-data rule
gate: QUEUED — lettering reads as a designed set (the #1 amateur-vs-pro tell) for 0 added nodes

technique: Real applied lettering is two stitched plies: a backing ply (the outline) + a top ply (the fill) with a satin-stitch edge. Added applique_top_ply — a thin crisp rim lighten(integratedFill,...) hugging the glyph exactly — so the eye reads two registered layers, not one stroked glyph. Rim stays in the fill color family so it never fights the contrast guard.
device_fps: NOT MEASURED — reasoned about cost per no-mock-data rule
gate: QUEUED — numerals/wordmarks read as designed two-ply applique; +1 node per label line at full size only, +0 at the most-numerous 34px thumbnail

technique: Replaced the generic diagonal sheen band with lightingProfile(variant): threaded variant into ModelDefs and tuned the SAME volume radial + sheen linear-gradient geometry per garment family (zero added nodes). football=broad soft low-gloss sheen high on the pad cap + higher/wider key (heavy twill); soccer/ucl=crisp tighter brighter streak (tech-poly); baseball=softest most diffuse lowest gloss (matte flannel); hockey=broad low diffuse (heavy knit); default cricket/tennis=balanced poly. Key falloff (volume peak) tuned per textile.
device_fps: NOT MEASURED — reasoned about cost per no-mock-data rule
gate: QUEUED — sheen now reads as one key light shaped to the actual textile, not a generic band, for 0 added nodes

technique: Audit found the whole trim/panel/stripe layer was still semi-transparent (fillOpacity 0.24-0.76) over the body while run-7 lettering was pure/full-opacity — so the secondary read washed/muddy. Lifted the MAJOR trim surfaces to near-solid (football yoke 0.34->0.82 + cuffs 0.46->0.92; hockey hem set 0.76->0.95/0.66->0.9/0.58->0.92 + accent stripes 0.34->0.62 + crest 0.66->0.9; basketball side panels 0.46/0.5->0.88/0.92 + hem trims 0.36/0.44->0.72/0.78; cricket sash 0.34->0.78 + collar/flashes/hem 0.42-0.72->0.82-0.9; tennis/soccer/ucl cuffs 0.34->0.78 + hems 0.52-0.54->0.82; baseball collar/piping/hem 0.5-0.92->0.78-1; LegalSafeCrest all 3 shapes 0.76-0.78->0.9). Left fold-highlight/AO/seam-stitch shading on top untouched so dimension is preserved. Trim gradient ramp shallower dark falloff (darken 0.26->0.2/0.24->0.18) so near-solid trim stays vivid end-to-end.

technique: readableDetail now ranks candidates BRAND-FIRST: true secondary/accent, then stronger tints of the SAME color (lighten/darken 0.34 then 0.55), then neutral white/near-black. Returns the richest brand candidate that clears 3.4:1 before falling back to a neutral, so PIT keeps near-black, LV/IND keep silver, CLE keeps an orange tint (all were over-snapping to white in run 7). ALSO fixed a real run-7 legibility bug: the old neutral fallback used a luminance>0.56 GUESS that could return the lower-contrast neutral on a mid-value body (scarlet #E31837: white=3.26, near-black=2.94 both miss 3.4) — replaced with a true highest-contrast pick over the full candidate set so the number is always the most legible the body allows.
node_delta: 0 (pure JS logic; candidate array 6->10 strings evaluated once per memoized mount, not render nodes)
device_fps: NOT MEASURED — pure color selection at mount, no render/per-frame cost
gate: QUEUED — more on-brand AND strictly more legible (closes run-7 next-target + fixes a fallback bug) for 0 nodes
